Q8 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of phrases given below should replace the phrase printed in bold type to make it grammatically correct?
Despite of their differences on matters of the principles, they all agree on the demand of hike in salary.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
“Despite their” in place of “Despite of their”. The preposition "of" is unnecessary after "despite".

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Choose an option, which can be substituted for the given phrase:
Something that can not be imitated.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(a)
Inimitable means incapable of being duplicated. The word duplicity means dishonest behavior that is intended to trick someone. Something that is impossible to explain is called inexplicable and something that is impossible to avoid or prevent is termed as inevitable.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
S1 : for skew tree, Ο(n)
S3 : Ο(n) for binary heap

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following finite automata M and N :

Let L be a language, such that contains 00 as substring}
Which of the above automata accepts L?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
M accepts (0 + 1)* 0 (0 + 1)* 0 (0 + 1)*, i.e. atleast two 0’s in string.
N accepts (0 + 1)* 00 (0 + 1)*, i.e. 00 is substring.

Q23 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following undirected simple graph G′ :

Which of the following are correct for G′ ?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
(b) B is before C, so D should before F.
(c) One DFS order is A, C, G, E, D, B, F. Here B is not parent of either G or D.
(d) In BFS, if source is fixed, then distance of a node from source is same in all possible BFS.

Not attempted

Which of the following are correct?

Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
(a) Depend upon signal, it can send to one thread or all threads.
(b) is false, because a user thread of a process cannot be in running state while the process itself is in the ready state.

Q49 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following languages are decidable. Note that <M> indicates encoding of the Turing Machine (TM) M

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
Use Rice’s theorem
(a) Every TM can converted to equivalent TM with odd number of states.
(b) L is undecidable because it’s impossible to count the number of accepted strings for an arbitrary TM.
(c) Steps are finite, so strings of that steps are also finite. So, run finite strings with finite steps is decidable.
(d) is undecidable because checking of a TM accepts any specific string is similar to the undecidable emptiness problem.
